_h2ai_validates: GAP-L1 Tiered Early Exit — framework exits on wave 0 or 1 when binary_checks pass; acceptance gate forces escalation when score is low
description: Draft a software vendor contract addendum for a SaaS agreement. The addendum must include all mandatory provisions required by CONSTRAINT-CL-1: (1) limitation of liability capped at 12 months of fees; (2) term-limited license (not perpetual); (3) GDPR Article 28 DPA reference; (4) 30-day cure period before termination for breach; (5) prohibition on unilateral amendment; (6) IP ownership assignment of custom deliverables to the customer. Write the addendum in formal legal language suitable for a commercial B2B SaaS contract. Each provision should be a clearly labeled section.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a commercial contracts attorney who has drafted hundreds of SaaS vendor agreements. You ensure every mandatory provision is present with precise legal language.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: Draft all six required provisions: (1) liability cap at 12 months fees, (2) term-limited license, (3) GDPR Art. 28 DPA reference, (4) 30-day cure period, (5) no unilateral amendment, (6) IP assignment to customer. Each must be a separate labeled section.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Any addendum missing even one of the six required provisions, or using perpetual license language, or failing to specify the 12-month liability cap explicitly.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a data protection officer who reviews vendor contracts for GDPR compliance. You verify that DPA obligations and IP assignments are correctly scoped.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: Ensure the GDPR Article 28 DPA reference is explicit and the IP ownership clause unambiguously assigns custom deliverable rights to the customer upon full payment.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Addendum that references data protection obligations vaguely without citing Article 28, or IP clause that retains vendor rights after payment.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a SaaS company's in-house counsel reviewing vendor addenda for enforceability. You focus on the liability cap, cure period, and amendment prohibition.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: Verify liability is capped at exactly 12 months of fees (not 6, not 24), cure period is exactly 30 days, and amendment requires written consent of both parties.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Wrong liability cap amount, cure period shorter than 30 days, or amendment clause that allows unilateral changes.

context: SaaS vendor contract addendum context: The main agreement is a standard SaaS subscription for a B2B software platform. The addendum modifies or supplements the main agreement on specific terms. All six provisions in CONSTRAINT-CL-1 are industry-standard requirements for enterprise SaaS contracts.

_review_checklist.text: Does the addendum contain a limitation-of-liability clause explicitly capping aggregate damages at twelve (12) months of fees paid?
Is the license grant term-limited (not perpetual), with an explicit end date or tie to the agreement term?
Does the addendum reference a Data Processing Agreement and state compliance with GDPR Article 28?
Does the addendum specify a 30-day written cure period before termination for breach?
Does the addendum explicitly prohibit unilateral amendment and require written consent of both parties?
Does the addendum contain an IP ownership assignment clause transferring custom deliverable rights to the customer upon full payment?
